title: Multiplication and division word problems (within 100)
description: Solve word problems involving multiplication and division. Solutions will be less than or equal to 100.

assessment_items.question_content:
There are $5$ spiders spinning webs under a porch. Each spider has $8$ legs. 

**How many legs do the spiders have all together?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] legs
hints:
We need to find the total number of legs for $\blueD5$ spiders.

We have $\blueD5$ groups of $\greenD8$ legs.

We can write this as $\blueD5 \times \greenD8$.

We can picture $5$ groups of $8$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

 $\blueD5 \times \greenD8 = ?$
The spiders have $40$ legs all together.

assessment_items.question_content:
After a game, Lilli's baseball team went out for ice cream. There are $9$ players on the team and they each ate $2$ scoops of ice cream.

**How many scoops of ice cream did the team eat?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] scoops
hints:
We need to find the total number of scoops for $\blueD9$ ice cream cones.

We have $\blueD9$ groups of $\greenD2$ scoops.

We can write this as $\blueD9 \times \greenD2$.

We can picture $9$ groups of $2$. 

[[☃ image 1]]



 $\blueD9 \times \greenD2 = ?$
The team ate $18$ scoops of ice cream.

assessment_items.question_content:
On one table at a book fair, there are $3$ rows of books with $6$ books in each row.

**What is the number of books on the table?**

[[☃ numeric-input 2]] books
hints:
We need to find the total number of books on the table.

We have $\blueD3$ groups of $\greenD6$ books.

We can write this as $\blueD3 \times \greenD6$.

We can picture $3$ groups of $6$. 


[[☃ image 1]]

 $\blueD3 \times \greenD6 = ?$
There are $18$ books on the table.

assessment_items.question_content:
George is making scrambled eggs for his family for breakfast. There are $4$ people in his family and each person can eat $2$ eggs.

**How many eggs should George cook all together?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] eggs
hints:
We need to find the total number of eggs that George should cook.

We have $\blueD4$ groups of $\greenD2$ eggs.

We can write this as $\blueD4 \times \greenD2$.

We can picture $4$ groups of $2$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

 $\blueD4 \times \greenD2 = ?$
George should cook $8$ eggs all together.

assessment_items.question_content:
A roller coaster has $4$ different cars. Each car holds $10$ people.

**How many people are on the roller coaster when all the cars are full?**

[[☃ numeric-input 2]] people
hints:
We need to find the total number of people that $4$ cars will hold.

We have $\blueD4$ groups of $\greenD{10}$ people.

We can write this as $\blueD4 \times \greenD{10}$.

We can picture $4$ groups of $10$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

 $\blueD4 \times \greenD{10} = ?$
There are $40$ people on the roller coaster when all the cars are full.

assessment_items.question_content:
A space museum has $6$ benches where people can sit to watch a short movie about Mars. Each bench holds $4$ people.

**What is the greatest number of people that can sit on the benches?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] people
hints:
We need to find the total number of people that $6$ benches will hold.

We have $\blueD6$ groups of $\greenD{4}$ people.

We can write this as $\blueD6 \times \greenD{4}$.

We can picture $6$ groups of $4$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

 $\blueD6 \times \greenD{4} = ?$
The greatest number of people who can sit on the benches is  $24$.

assessment_items.question_content:
An art teacher has $6$ boxes of chalk to draw an outdoor mural. Each box has $7$ pieces of chalk.

**How many pieces of chalk does the art teacher have?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] pieces
hints:
We need to find the total number of  pieces of chalk in $6$ boxes.

We have $\blueD6$ groups of $\greenD{7}$ pieces of chalk.

We can write this as $\blueD6 \times \greenD{7}$.

We can picture $6$ groups of $7$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

 $\blueD6 \times \greenD{7} = ?$
The art teacher has $42$ pieces of chalk.

assessment_items.question_content:
Felix took $8$ pictures each day during his family's $7$-day trip to the Grand Canyon.

**How many pictures did Felix take all together?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] pictures
hints:
We need to find the total number of  pictures in $7$ days.

We have $\blueD7$ groups of $\greenD{8}$ pictures.

We can write this as $\blueD7 \times \greenD{8}$.

We can picture $7$ groups of $8$. 


[[☃ image 1]]

 $\blueD7 \times \greenD{8} = ?$
Felix took $56$ pictures all together.

assessment_items.question_content:
A movie theater shows $9$ minutes of previews before a movie starts. Each preview lasts $3$ minutes.

**How many previews does the theater show?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] previews
hints:
There are $\greenD{9}$ minutes of previews to divide into groups with $\blueD3$ minutes in each group.

We can write this as $\greenD{9} \div \blueD3$.

We can picture $\greenD{9}$ divided into groups with $\blueD3$ minutes in each group.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\greenD{9} \div\blueD3 = ?$
The theater shows $3$ previews.

assessment_items.question_content:
For field day, Mr. Cotman has a total of $49$ water balloons.  He gives each group $7$ balloons.

**How many groups does Mr. Cotman give balloons to?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] groups
hints:
There are $\greenD{49}$ balloons to divide into groups with $\blueD7$ balloons in each group.

We can write this as $\greenD{49} \div \blueD7$.

We can picture $\greenD{49}$ divided into groups with $\blueD7$ balloons in each group.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\greenD{49} \div\blueD7 = ?$
Mr. Cotman gives balloons to $7$ groups.

assessment_items.question_content:
A gym teacher splits $48$ students into groups of $8$ students to play basketball.

**How many groups of students are playing basketball?**

[[☃ numeric-input 2]] groups
hints:
There are $\greenD{48}$ students to divide into groups with $\blueD8$ students in each group.

We can write this as $\greenD{48} \div \blueD8$.

[[☃ image 1]]

We can picture $\greenD{48}$ divided into groups with $\blueD8$ students in each group.


$\greenD{48} \div\blueD8 = ?$
There are $6$ groups of students playing basketball.

assessment_items.question_content:
A frog needs to jump $21$ feet before she can reach her favorite pond.  Each jump covers $3$ feet.

**How many times will the frog need to jump to reach the pond?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] times
hints:
There are $\greenD{21}$ feet to divide into groups with $\blueD3$ feet in each group.

We can write this as $\greenD{21} \div \blueD3$.

We can picture $\greenD{21}$ feet divided into groups with $\blueD3$ feet in each group.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\greenD{21} \div\blueD3 = ?$
The frog will need to jump $7$ times to reach the pond.

assessment_items.question_content:
Anna has $32$ pages left to read in her chapter book. She plans to read $8$ pages each night until she is finished.

**How many nights will it take Anna to finish the book?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] nights
hints:
There are $\greenD{32}$ pages to divide evenly into  groups with $\blueD8$ pages in each group.

We can write this as $\greenD{32} \div \blueD8$.

We can picture $\greenD{32}$ divided into groups with $\blueD8$ pages in each group.


[[☃ image 1]]

$\greenD{32} \div\blueD8 = ?$
It will take Anna $4$ nights to finish her book.

assessment_items.question_content:
Sidra and her sister have $20$ minutes to play with their new hula hoop before dinner.  They  split the time evenly.

**How long will they each get to play with the hula hoop?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] minutes
hints:
There are $\greenD{20}$ minutes of play time to divide evenly between $\blueD2$ people.

We can write this as $\greenD{20} \div \blueD2$.

We can picture $\greenD{20}$ divided into $\blueD2$ equal-sized groups.  

[[☃ image 1]]

$\greenD{20} \div\blueD2 = ?$
Sidra and her sister will each get to play with the hula hoop for $10$ minutes.

assessment_items.question_content:
Jenny spent $36$ minutes practicing $4$ songs on her violin. She spent the same amount of time on each song.

**How long did Jenny spend practicing each song?**

[[☃ numeric-input 2]] minutes
hints:
There are $\greenD{36}$ minutes to divide evenly between  $\blueD4$ songs.

We can write this as $\greenD{36} \div \blueD4$.

We can picture $\greenD{36}$ divided into $\blueD4$ equal-sized groups.  



[[☃ image 1]]

$\greenD{36} \div\blueD4 = ?$
Jenny spent $9$ minutes practicing each song.

assessment_items.question_content:
To start a card game, $36$ cards are dealt equally to $6$ players.

**How many cards does each player get?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] cards
hints:
There are $\greenD{36}$ cards to divide evenly between $\blueD6$ players.

We can write this as $\greenD{36} \div \blueD6$.

We can picture $\greenD{36}$ divided into $\blueD6$ equal-sized groups.  


[[☃ image 1]]

$\greenD{36} \div\blueD6 = ?$
Each player gets $6$ cards.
